assessment_items.question_content:
**Pilgrimage stimulated the local economy for all of the following reasons EXCEPT . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Pilgrimage stimulated the economy, as pilgrims needed places to stay, places to eat, and places to buy souvenirs.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/romanesque/v/tympanum-of-the-last-judgment--autun)**

choices.content: pilgrims needed to pay entrance fees to the churches

assessment_items.question_content:
**Images of heaven and hell caused medieval people to consider their [[☃ dropdown 1]].**

hints:
**Images of heaven and hell caused medieval people to consider their eternal fate.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of the Last Judgment tympanum, St. Lazare, pilgrimages, or relics?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f6832d94521da8deabe68a61dea43448.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Relics held spiritual power to effect healing miracles and time off from Purgatory. In Catholic belief, Purgatory is a place of waiting and purification before a soul can enter heaven.**  

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/romanesque/v/tympanum-of-the-last-judgment--autun)**

choices.content: This Last Judgment tympanum in Autun was one of the first monumental sculptures since antiquity.
As pilgrimages became increasingly popular, there was a building boom in Europe.
The Cathedral of St. Lazare held the relics, or bones, of St. Lazarus.
Relics held spiritual power to get sinners out of hell and immediately into heaven.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is not true of the central figure of Christ in the Last Judgment?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Christ appears here in judgment, sending sinners to hell and the blessed to heaven. He is not kind and sympathetic.**

choices.content: he appears flattened, frontal and symmetrical
his body proportions are not naturalistic
he stares out kindly and sympathetically

assessment_items.question_content:
**The almond shape that surrounds Christ is called a $\_\_\_\_\_\_$.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b58dd861fd0fa44899bd36940ed07961.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The almond shape that surrounds the figure of Christ is known as a mandorla, and it indicates his divinity.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The man named Gislebertus mentioned in the inscription is the name of the artist responsible for carving the tympanum.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **It would be extremely unusual if the name in the inscription referred to the name of the artist since in the medieval period, artists were considered craftsman, they were not seen as individual geniuses. Recent scholarship that suggests that Gislebertus is the name of a Duke who was associated with bringing the bones of St. Lazarus to Autun.**
